svg.title: 30 Panel HUB75 LED Wall Build Schematic
svg.desc: Schematic showing 30 HUB75 RGB LED panels in a 5 wide by 6 high array mounted with magnetic screws to vertical steel strips on a rigid backer board, with a symmetric front-removable PETG bezel, rear-mounted 5V power supplies, fused distribution, and Raspberry Pi HUB75 signal chains.
svg.text: 5×6 array (5 wide, 6 high), 4.20 ft × 2.52 ft, 640×384 px, magnetic screws on vertical steel strips, symmetric removable PETG bezel on backer margin, rear-mounted power/controller
Front View: Scaled Symmetric Bezel Around 5×6 Panel Array
Panels: 1280 mm × 768 mm. Bezel shown at ~30 mm wide: visible, removable, and mounted to exposed backer margin.

g.text: Backer extends beyond panels; bezel screws into exposed margin.

svg.text: Backer/front-face plan before panels are installed. 6 strips run top-to-bottom, centered on each panel-column edge: left edge, 4 seams, right edge.

g.text: Each strip is centered on a column edge so it serves both adjacent panel columns; strips never overlap each other or the bezel inserts.

svg.text: Depth Cross-Section: Panel, Magnet, Steel, Backer, PETG Bezel
Cut through the left outside edge. Front is left; wall/backer is right. The bezel is a Z/L profile screwed into the backer margin.

g.text: Front/viewer side ← left; rear/wall side → right.
Bezel screws are accessible from the front. Remove bezel segment first, then pull edge panel off magnets.
Use black PETG/ASA segments with the same profile on all sides.
Use slotted screw holes for 1-2mm adjustment.
Backer margin target: 25-40mm outside panel array.
Do not hide screws behind panels; screws must be front-removable.
1. Remove the needed bezel segment from the front.
2. Pull the edge panel off magnetic screws.
4. Reinstall panel; magnets reseat against steel strips.
5. Reinstall bezel segment with slotted adjustment.

svg.text: Rear View: Power Zones, Data Chains, and Mounting Pattern
Four 60A supplies feed four zones (9/6/9/6 panels). Grounds are common; +5V stays separated by zone unless using current-sharing hardware.

g.text: Steel strip plan: 6 vertical strips: left edge, 4 shared column seams, right edge. 2" wide required: inserts ~7.5mm off-edge, magnet feet ~Ø20mm → 35mm span + tolerance.
Snake each chain across two rows of five
• 30 × P2 indoor HUB75/HUB75E RGB panels, 256mm × 128mm each, 128×64 px each, ICN6373C + ICN32019 chipset, 1/32 scan
• 120 × M3/M4 LED-module magnetic screws, 15mm standoff height, 4 per panel; confirm M3 vs M4 and thread depth before bulk order
• 1 × 1/2" plywood or MDF backer, approx 1360mm × 850mm target to allow 25–40mm bezel/backer margin around panel array
• Rear stiffness/wall standoff frame: 1×2 lumber, plywood ribs, or 2020 extrusion; provides cleat mount, airflow, and PSU clearance
• 6 × vertical mild-steel strips, 1/16" thick, 2" (50.8mm) wide; each approx 840mm (33in) long: 768mm array height + bezel-margin overrun
• Steel total length: 6 × 840mm = 5040mm / 5.04m / 16.5ft; buy three 8ft strips (two strips per stick) with offcuts for landing plates/mistakes
• PETG or ASA printed bezel, segmented with max printed part length ≤10in; black, removable, magnet-attached or screw-attached to exposed margin
• 4 × Mean Well LRS-350-5 or equivalent 5V 60A supplies; 3 is the strict minimum (~173A max load) but leaves only ~7A headroom
• 30 × HUB75 16-pin IDC ribbon cables, one panel-to-panel/controller cable per module position; order extras in short lengths
• 30 × 5V panel power pigtails/cables, one per panel; avoid relying on high-current daisy chains
• 1 × surge protector or switched/fused AC power strip rated for total AC load; use grounded mains wiring and strain relief
• DC distribution: fused distribution blocks or busbar + fuse block per PSU zone; split into 4 zones (9/6/9/6 panels)
• Suggested fusing: one fuse per panel or small group; size based on wire gauge and branch load, not just panel max draw
• Wire: short 5V/GND branches sized for current and voltage drop; common ground across PSUs, do not tie +5V outputs together directly
• Vendor listing specs: 256×128mm, 128×64 px, P2, 1/32 scan, 5V, ICN6373C + ICN32019. Confirm HUB75E pinout, max watts, and M3/M4 insert size with seller.
• Magnetic screw standoff target: 15mm unless one-panel test shows lower profile clears ribs/connectors and cable bend radius.
• Steel strips extend ~25–40mm past the panel array top and bottom into the bezel margins for magnetic bezel attachment; sides get separate landing plates.
